content: Global health burden assessment methodology: (1) Mortality data—vital registration systems, verbal autopsy for settings without death certification, sibling history surveys, demographic surveillance sites; (2) Morbidity data—household surveys (DHS, MICS), hospital records, disease registries, claims data, published literature systematic reviews; (3) Disability weights—population surveys valuing health states, expert panels, patient-reported outcomes; (4) Risk factor measurement—household surveys (STEPS), environmental monitoring, sales data, comparative risk assessment framework; (5) Statistical methods—DisMod-MR disease modeling, Cause of Death Ensemble modeling (CODEm), spatiotemporal Gaussian process regression, uncertainty quantification through Monte Carlo simulation; (6) Attribution analyses—population attributable fractions for risk factors, counterfactual scenarios. Limitations include data gaps (especially mental health, rare diseases), measurement error in low-resource settings, disability weight cultural variability, and attribution uncertainty for multimorbidity. GBD results updated annually with methodological improvements.
